hypothesis: Incorporating an uncertainty-weighted physics feedback signal into Direct Preference Optimization, by scaling Thermo-Calc rewards with the predicted confidence of phase predictions (e.g., via ensemble CALPHAD or multi-temperature evaluations), will yield higher-coverage and higher-validity BCC/B2 alloy candidates and more robust DPO performance across elements than standard DPO with a fixed reward, particularly in composition regions with high simulation uncertainty.
